test_title: Take tests to get a personal recommendation
test_subtitle:
Choose one of the options:
1. Our recommended set: 3 required + 2 optional
2. Build your own set: choose up to 4 tests from the list
test_variant2Title: Option 2 — Build your own set (maximum {max} tests)
test_toastMaxTests: You can select no more than {max} tests
test_recommended_subtitle: 5 tests for the most accurate result
test_why_1_text: What you enjoy and where you have a natural inclination.
test_why_2_text: How you think, make decisions, and interact with people.
test_why_3_text: What matters to you in work: money, meaning, stability, or growth.
test_why_4_text: What you already do well and what can be developed for your career.
test_why_conclusion: We analyze you from 4 angles → precise profession recommendation
test_result_5: Where to study and what steps to take next
test_custom_warning: ⚠ The more tests you take, the more accurate the result.
hero_subtitle: Take several science‑based tests and get a personal career analysis powered by AI
hero_prof1_desc: Working with data, visualization, and decision‑making based on analytics.
hero_prof3_desc: Building applications, programming, working in a team.
how_card1_desc: tests for interests, personality, values, and skills
how_card2_subtitle: we combine results and find patterns
how_step1_desc: We will determine your interests, thinking style, and strengths
how_step1_extra: Holland • Photo test • DiSC • Personality • Values
how_step2_desc: The algorithm analyzes your profile and selects suitable professions
how_step3_desc: Get a list of professions, skills, and where to study
tests_subtitle: Hover over a card to pause the carousel
tests_carousel_subtitle: Take the tests — we will pick professions that fit you
tests_holland_features:
6 environment types: Realistic, Investigative, Artistic, Social, Enterprising, Conventional
Questions about interests in activities and careers
3‑letter code (for example, RIA) — your interest profile
Interpretation by types and suggested careers
Science‑based model, used in career counseling worldwide
~15 minutes
tests_photo-career_features:
Visual test: choose pictures instead of text questions
Fast and clear even without language proficiency
Linked with Holland Code — result in RIASEC format
Great for visual learners
Less fatigue while taking the test
~10 minutes
tests_disc_features:
4 types: Dominance, Influence, Steadiness, Conscientiousness
Communication and teamwork style
Popular with employers for assessing employees
Helps you understand how you influence and respond to others
Useful for leadership and team work
~12 minutes
tests_career-aptitude_features:
Combined career assessment based on interests and personality
54 questions about concrete work activities (RIASEC)
+ 40 personality statements (Big Five subset)
Shows which fields fit your natural talents and preferences
Helps choose directions to explore in more depth
~10–15 minutes
tests_big-five_features:
5 traits: Openness, Conscientiousness, Extraversion, Agreeableness, Neuroticism
74 items in three formats (sentences, words, extended items)
Science‑based Five‑Factor Model (OCEAN)
Balanced positive/negative phrasing and reverse‑scored items
Great base test to understand your overall personality profile
~8–12 minutes
tests_eq_features:
Measures global EQ and five key skills: self‑awareness, social awareness, emotional control, empathy, well‑being
Shows how you handle your own and others’ emotions
Highlights strengths and growth areas for relationships and work
Objective baseline for developing emotional skills over time
55 questions, ~5–10 minutes
tests_enneagram_features:
9 core personality types based on motivations, fears and coping patterns
Includes triads (Head/Heart/Body), wings and arrows
Helps understand why you react the way you do
Useful for deep self‑knowledge, relationships and personal growth
Results: primary type, likely wing and growth directions
~10–15 minutes
tests_typefinder-16_features:
Measures four Myers & Briggs dimensions (I/E, S/N, T/F, J/P)
Assigns a four‑letter type code like INFJ or ESTP
Explains your energy, decision‑making and work style
Good for team communication and career choice
Modern implementation of the 16 personalities model
~10–15 minutes
tests_strengths_features:
Find your top 5 personal strengths
Highlights the traits that shape how you think, feel, and act
Helps you make more aligned decisions and build on what comes naturally
Includes a free basic report
Results: ranked Top‑5 strengths with descriptions
~10 minutes
stats_subtitle: Up‑to‑date data on the labor market and education in Kazakhstan
section1_chip2: AI will explain what fits you
aichat_subtitle: Ask a question about your results — get a clear answer
aichat_example_user: Why is analytics a good fit for me?
aichat_example_ai: Because your interest in data and logical thinking fits this field well. In the values test you prioritize structure and growth — analytics offers both. I recommend starting with SQL and data visualization courses.
footer_copyright: © {year} CareerMap. Career guidance for school and university students in Kazakhstan.
holland_subtitle: Answer all questions honestly. The results will help identify your career interests.
holland_desc_R: Doer who works with hands and tools
holland_desc_A: Creator who expresses themselves through art
disc_subtitle: Evaluate statements to understand your communication and behavior style.
disc_pairs_title: In each pair, select the word that best describes you.
disc_single_title: Rate how much each word is like you.
bigfive_subtitle: Rate how accurately each statement describes you.
career_part1_subtitle: Mark your interest in each activity shown.
career_part2_subtitle: Rate how accurately each statement describes you.
career_part1_instruction: Don't worry about skills or salary — simply think about whether you would enjoy doing it.
career_part2_instruction: Mark your choice based on how accurate each statement is for you.
photo_subtitle: Select the image that best represents the job you would prefer
photo_interpretationText: Based on your visual preferences, you showed the highest interest in {category1} ({percent1}%), {category2} ({percent2}%), and {category3} ({percent3}%). This corresponds to the Holland Code {code}, which points to careers related to {desc1}, {desc2}, and {desc3}.
register_password_helper: At least 8 characters. Recommended: mixed case letters and numbers.
register_error_length: Password must be at least 8 characters long.
